exercises.title: Confusion Matrix Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze readout data to construct confusion matrix and calculate assignment fidelity.

# Simulated readout data
np.random.seed(42)
n_shots = 10000

# P(1|0) = 0.02, P(0|1) = 0.04 (asymmetric errors)
prep_0_results = np.random.choice([0, 1], size=n_shots, p=[0.98, 0.02])
prep_1_results = np.random.choice([0, 1], size=n_shots, p=[0.04, 0.96])

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def construct_confusion_matrix(prep_0_results, prep_1_results):
    """
    Construct confusion matrix from measurement results.
    """
    n_0 = len(prep_0_results)
    n_1 = len(prep_1_results)
    
    # Count outcomes
    p_0_given_0 = np.sum(prep_0_results == 0) / n_0
    p_1_given_0 = np.sum(prep_0_results == 1) / n_0
    p_0_given_1 = np.sum(prep_1_results == 0) / n_1
    p_1_given_1 = np.sum(prep_1_results == 1) / n_1
    
    confusion = np.array([
        [p_0_given_0, p_0_given_1],
        [p_1_given_0, p_1_given_1]
    ])
    
    return confusion

def assignment_fidelity(confusion_matrix):
    """Calculate assignment fidelity."""
    return np.mean(np.diag(confusion_matrix))

def calculate_snr(confusion_matrix):
    """Calculate signal-to-noise ratio."""
    # Simplified: based on diagonal vs off-diagonal
    p00, p01 = confusion_matrix[0]
    p10, p11 = confusion_matrix[1]
    
    # SNR ~ (correct - incorrect) / sqrt(variance)
    signal = (p00 + p11) - (p01 + p10)
    noise = np.sqrt(p01 * p10 + 0.01)  # Approximate
    
    return signal / noise

# Simulated readout data
np.random.seed(42)
n_shots = 10000

prep_0_results = np.random.choice([0, 1], size=n_shots, p=[0.98, 0.02])
prep_1_results = np.random.choice([0, 1], size=n_shots, p=[0.04, 0.96])

confusion = construct_confusion_matrix(prep_0_results, prep_1_results)
fidelity = assignment_fidelity(confusion)
snr = calculate_snr(confusion)

print("Readout Characterization Results")
print("="*40)
print("\nConfusion Matrix:")
print(f"           Prep |0>    Prep |1>")
print(f"  Meas 0:  {confusion[0,0]:.4f}     {confusion[0,1]:.4f}")
print(f"  Meas 1:  {confusion[1,0]:.4f}     {confusion[1,1]:.4f}")
print(f"\nAssignment Fidelity: {fidelity*100:.2f}%")
print(f"P(correct|0): {confusion[0,0]*100:.2f}%")
print(f"P(correct|1): {confusion[1,1]*100:.2f}%")
print(f"False positive P(1|0): {confusion[1,0]*100:.2f}%")
print(f"False negative P(0|1): {confusion[0,1]*100:.2f}%")
hints: M[m,n] = (# times measured m when prepared n) / (# times prepared n)
Assignment fidelity is the average of diagonal elements
Columns of confusion matrix should sum to 1

exercises.title: Readout Error Mitigation
exercises.description: Implement readout error mitigation for single and multi-qubit systems.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def mitigate_single_qubit(measured_probs, confusion_matrix):
    """
    Mitigate readout errors for single qubit.
    
    Args:
        measured_probs: [P(0), P(1)] from experiment
        confusion_matrix: 2x2 readout confusion matrix
    
    Returns:
        Corrected probabilities
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def mitigate_multi_qubit(measured_probs, single_qubit_confusions):
    """
    Mitigate readout errors for multi-qubit system.
    
    Args:
        measured_probs: 2^n array of measured probabilities
        single_qubit_confusions: List of 2x2 confusion matrices
    
    Returns:
        Corrected probabilities
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Single qubit example
confusion_1q = np.array([[0.98, 0.03],
                         [0.02, 0.97]])

# True state should be 70% |0>, 30% |1>
# But with errors, we measure:
measured_1q = np.array([0.72, 0.28])  # Slightly biased

corrected_1q = mitigate_single_qubit(measured_1q, confusion_1q)
print(f"Single qubit:")
print(f"  Measured: P(0)={measured_1q[0]:.3f}, P(1)={measured_1q[1]:.3f}")
print(f"  Corrected: P(0)={corrected_1q[0]:.3f}, P(1)={corrected_1q[1]:.3f}")

# Two qubit example
confusion_q0 = np.array([[0.97, 0.04], [0.03, 0.96]])
confusion_q1 = np.array([[0.98, 0.02], [0.02, 0.98]])

# True Bell state: 50% |00>, 50% |11>
measured_2q = np.array([0.48, 0.03, 0.02, 0.47])  # With errors

corrected_2q = mitigate_multi_qubit(measured_2q, [confusion_q0, confusion_q1])
print(f"\nTwo qubit:")
print(f"  Measured: {measured_2q}")
print(f"  Corrected: {corrected_2q}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def mitigate_single_qubit(measured_probs, confusion_matrix):
    """
    Mitigate readout errors for single qubit.
    """
    # Invert confusion matrix
    M_inv = np.linalg.inv(confusion_matrix)
    
    # Apply correction
    corrected = M_inv @ measured_probs
    
    # Clip to valid range and renormalize
    corrected = np.clip(corrected, 0, 1)
    corrected = corrected / np.sum(corrected)
    
    return corrected

def mitigate_multi_qubit(measured_probs, single_qubit_confusions):
    """
    Mitigate readout errors for multi-qubit system.
    """
    # Build full confusion matrix via tensor product
    full_confusion = single_qubit_confusions[0]
    for conf in single_qubit_confusions[1:]:
        full_confusion = np.kron(full_confusion, conf)
    
    # Invert and apply
    M_inv = np.linalg.inv(full_confusion)
    corrected = M_inv @ measured_probs
    
    # Clip and renormalize
    corrected = np.clip(corrected, 0, 1)
    corrected = corrected / np.sum(corrected)
    
    return corrected

# Single qubit example
confusion_1q = np.array([[0.98, 0.03],
                         [0.02, 0.97]])

measured_1q = np.array([0.72, 0.28])
corrected_1q = mitigate_single_qubit(measured_1q, confusion_1q)

print("Single Qubit Mitigation")
print("="*40)
print(f"  Measured: P(0)={measured_1q[0]:.4f}, P(1)={measured_1q[1]:.4f}")
print(f"  Corrected: P(0)={corrected_1q[0]:.4f}, P(1)={corrected_1q[1]:.4f}")

# Two qubit example
confusion_q0 = np.array([[0.97, 0.04], [0.03, 0.96]])
confusion_q1 = np.array([[0.98, 0.02], [0.02, 0.98]])

measured_2q = np.array([0.48, 0.03, 0.02, 0.47])
corrected_2q = mitigate_multi_qubit(measured_2q, [confusion_q0, confusion_q1])

print("\nTwo Qubit Mitigation")
print("="*40)
print("State | Measured | Corrected")
print("------|----------|----------")
for i, state in enumerate(['00', '01', '10', '11']):
    print(f" |{state}> |  {measured_2q[i]:.4f}  |  {corrected_2q[i]:.4f}")

print(f"\nBell state fidelity (P(00)+P(11)):")
print(f"  Measured: {measured_2q[0]+measured_2q[3]:.4f}")
print(f"  Corrected: {corrected_2q[0]+corrected_2q[3]:.4f}")
hints: For single qubit: p_true = M^(-1) @ p_measured
For multi-qubit: M_full = M_1 tensor M_2 tensor ...
Always clip corrected probabilities to [0,1] and renormalize
